diagrams.title: 5-Stage RISC Pipeline Structure
diagrams.description: Shows the standard five-stage pipeline (Instruction Fetch, Instruction Decode, Execute, Memory Access, Write Back) with instructions flowing through each stage in parallel. This establishes the baseline pipeline architecture needed to understand how interrupts disrupt the flow.
elements: Pipeline stages (IF, ID, EX, MEM, WB)
diagrams.purpose: Explains the baseline pipeline architecture before introducing interrupt disruption

diagrams.title: Pipeline Flush on Interrupt Detection
diagrams.description: Illustrates what happens when an interrupt is detected during the Execute stage. Shows all in-flight instructions being invalidated (flushed) from pipeline stages after the interrupt point, and the PC being saved for resuming. Demonstrates the penalty of pipeline clearing.
diagrams.purpose: Visualizes how interrupts cause pipeline disruption and the state that must be saved

diagrams.title: Interrupt Latency and Return Sequence
diagrams.description: Timeline diagram showing the complete interrupt handling sequence: from interrupt detection through ISR execution to pipeline restoration. Shows the cycles lost to flush, the ISR execution, and the refetch of the interrupted instruction.
diagrams.purpose: Explains interrupt latency concepts and the full interrupt handler lifecycle in a pipelined processor
